title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Timeout Layers
summary: A health aggregator has a 900 ms caller deadline spanning DNS, TLS, headers, streamed content, parsing, and cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go timeout layers increment in a Go 1.26 net/http end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Choose revalidation, stale serving, explicit invalidation, or bypass while preserving correctness during failure. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
